title: CI: use z1d instance for windows machine testing
body:
This one has two local NVMe's attached so we should be able to use fast
local storage to hopefully speed the test up. The NVMe are not mounted
by default so we have to format and mount them. I have choosen Z as
drive letter as I guess it seems most likely that it is free.
Then we need to set the TMPDIR envs to make the machine tests use the
new location.

comments.body:
@baude @l0rd PTAL

Speed up is around 20 mins total run time from around 47 mins to 27 mins so a lot.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *l0rd*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,l0rd] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Despite the PR title the change affects both WSL and Hyper-V tests right? And we are limiting it to the e2e test task, not for unit tests?

Yes I mean the motivation was to speed up hyperV, looking at the WSL times we also speed it up as well by ~9mins so I can update the commit message.

Unit tests should not need it, they should not be IO bound and these metal instances are more expensive so we should use smaller instances where possible. Unit tests take like 6-7 mins so that is totally fine.

> > Despite the PR title the change affects both WSL and Hyper-V tests right? And we are limiting it to the e2e test task, not for unit tests?
> 
> Yes I mean the motivation was to speed up hyperV, looking at the WSL times we also speed it up as well by ~9mins so I can update the commit message.

> Unit tests should not need it, they should not be IO bound and these metal instances are more expensive so we should use smaller instances where possible. Unit tests take like 6-7 mins so that is totally fine.

Makes sense. You probably want to change your commit message for clarity then.
